decisions.detail: Drift corrected: 5 Shorts were stale SCHEDULED in state but verified PUBLIC via YouTube oEmbed (HTTP 200) — v2_twist(vxP6XiJKLkg, 06-05), v2_answer(TwJNypz8c4I, 06-06), v3_hook(5e8ELVo5ARg, 06-08), v3_twist(0eDPVcgODvY, 06-10), v3_answer(y3xLIfOAPHA, 06-12). Set status=PUBLIC + actual_published_at=scheduled_at for each. Cross-checked: live voidline Studio session sitting on 5e8ELVo5ARg analytics period-since_publish.

Pipeline empty: no Shorts scheduled for next 3 days (Sun 06-14, Mon 06-15, Tue 06-16). Catalogue queue is dry beyond v3_answer (06-12) — the entire v3 Tunguska batch is now published with nothing staged behind it. Cadence target is 5 Shorts/wk (Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun) so 06-14 + 06-15 slots are already unfilled. ACTION NEEDED: render+schedule next batch (v4 topic). Also: tomorrow Sun 06-14 17:00 UTC is a long-form + Reddit-seed day per cadence, but no long-form appears scheduled — verify in tomorrow's run.

Drift corrected: v1_bonus_briggs (vZ68HlWfT-Q, scheduled 2026-06-15 12:00 UTC) was still stale SCHEDULED in state but verified PUBLIC via YouTube oEmbed (HTTP 200, title "Why Did the Teetotal Captain Run? — Mary Celeste 1872", author Voidline). Set status=PUBLIC + scheduled_at + actual_published_at=2026-06-15T12:00:00Z. Same auto-publish-not-reflected-in-state pattern as the 06-13 reconcile.

PIPELINE FULLY DRY for 14 days. Last upload = v1_bonus_briggs published 2026-06-15; nothing scheduled since. 0 Shorts in state for today (Mon 06-29, a HOOK-Short day per recovery cadence) or the next 3 days (Tue 06-30, Wed 07-01 = ANSWER day, Thu 07-02). No SCHEDULED items remain at all. Next long-form slot Sun 07-05 17:00 UTC has nothing queued. v4 Roanoke remains blocked on the Google Flow Nano Banana 2 thumb (disabled-submit blocker from 06-13, never resolved). This now violates KNOWN_BAD 'Cadence <1 video/week → algo can't categorize the channel' — the channel has been silent ~2 weeks. ACTION NEEDED: unblock v4 Flow thumb (fresh-session Flow inspect OR ship with v3 forest base placeholder) + render/schedule next Short batch before rhythm fully breaks.
